repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

p.ref: Series Ib. On Our Backs photographs

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
list.item: The Blatant Image: A Magazine of Feminist Photography, no. 2, 1982 ( with 2 images by Honey Lee Cottrell and a note by her on the inside cover. Self-portrait on p. 16 and her portrait of Jeanette Foster on p. 30.)
Gayle, Marilyn What Lesbians Do, 1975 (inscribed and signed by the author and artist Barbary)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Honey Lee Cottrell Papers, #7822. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Digital scans of some photographs are also available through the digital portal.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Honey Lee Cottrell (1946-2015) was a noted photographer who helped define lesbian erotica in the 1980s through her contributions to the women.s sex magazine On Our Backs over its first seven years. She studied at the National Sex Forum and was a volunteer switchboard operator at San Francisco Sex Information. Her photographs appeared in I Am My Lover (1979), Coming to Power (1981), A Dykes Bike Repair Handbook (1990), and Nothing But the Girl (1996), as well as in magazines including The Blatant Image and Sinister Wisdom. In 1981, Cottrell received a BA in film studies from San Francisco State University. She was director and camera for Sweet Dreams starring Pat Califia (National Sex Forum, 1980), and from 1985 to the early 90s, a cinematographer for Fatale Video, the first lesbian-created erotic movie company. In the late 1980s and early 1990s, she also collaborated on erotic and sex education videos of interest to lesbians and others produced by S.I.R. (Sex Indulgence & Rock.n Roll), a video production company run by Shar Rednour and Jackie Strano. She took photos of Susie Bright and Shar Rednour for their book covers. She photographed her lovers and friends and documented queer and kink cultures for decades with her first camera, a 35 mm Nikkormat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Personal papers and photographic archives. These include many test prints, notes about how to print particular images, items collected for collage projects, event publicity flyers, and files of collaboration with Tee Corinne, Susie Bright, Joani Blank, Shar Rednour, Jackie Strano, Phyllis Christopher (friend and staff photographer for the periodical On Our Backs), and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also, 19 black and white photos by Honey Lee Cottrell, 4 strips of contact prints, and a Tee Corinne image possibly printed by Cottrell. Subjects include Seeley, lesbians on California whitewater rafting trips organized by Seeley, Maxine Feldman (San Francisco, March 1977), and 2 images from Seeley and Cottrell's trip to Europe. Also, 4 color photographs by Marsha Seeley of Cottrell and Maxine Feldman, San Francisco, March 1977.

did.unittitle: Cottrell's handwritten list of Erotic Portraits
Erotic Portraits: Gayle Rubin and Pat Califia
Erotic Portraits: Amber Hollibaugh, Jim Garver of the National Sex Forum, time of the Barnard Conference on Sexuality

scopecontent.p: Includes negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first two transmen who agreed/asked to be photographed and interviewed for OOB. Includes "Rites of Passage" Newsletter, Volume 1 Number 1.

Stripper Jessica and Elexis, Rachael and Elexis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains prints and contact sheets from the photo shoot that produced the widely published photograph of Rachael Williams and Elexis, "Coastside Train."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First OOB photo showing nude male.

Nina and Bobby- The Hunt layouts, letter about the story

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Given to Curator Brenda Marston near the time of her death.

Includes photo of Honey Lee playing the harmonica as a child; self-portraits, other photographs and collages by Honey Lee Cottrell; photographs by Tee Corinne; letterhead for Duanne and Patricia in Leslie, Michigan; photographs of Gayle Rubin, Pat Califia, Amber Hollibaugh, Tee Corinne, and unlabelled photographs.

scopecontent.p: Raelynn Gallina died of cancer about a year before Honey, and was the first serious lesbian piercer

scopecontent.p: Gerald was a Chinese businessman Honey Lee married as a green card favor

did.unittitle: The Memorial at Castro Street and 18th street (Hibernia Beach)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Where gay men paid a huge tribute to Lucille Ball on occasion of her death. One of the last grand "old gay" gestures in the Castro."

did.unittitle: Tee Corinne photo to Honey Lee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "From Austin, she came to SF to be a film-maker. The only one of HL's Famous dykes who didn't become famous."

did.unittitle: Jen di Gioa and Aretha, Folsum St. Fair

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes rubbing of family plaque

did.unittitle: Slides: Susie Promo Chromes, Summer '99 and others
Honey Lee Self portrait, SB Snapped
Birdie Yusbu, Golden Gate Park, San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roberta "Birdie" Yusba, an important founding member of the SF Gay and Lesbian History Project who researched and created an educational slideshow about lesbian-themed pulp novels

did.unittitle: Nan Kenney in front of St. Peter's in North Beach, San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph taken the same year Kinney played the cashier in Monika Treut's movie Die Jungfrauen Maschine (Virgin Machine).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rachael Williams, a Black dyke who in 1981 became the first Ms. Leather and the first Black person to hold the equivalent of a national title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes signed prints and a black and white photograph of 1985 Coastside Train photoshoot with Elexis posing on a freight train (one additional photo from this shoot appears in collection 7788, Susie Bright Papers, Box 20 Folder 29)

Cunt Coloring Book, drawings by Tee Corinne

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies, one with signatures and notes

did.unittitle: Interview of Honey Lee Cottrell by Brenda Marston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of full recording: On Sunday, September 6, 2015, Honey Lee Cottrell and Brenda Marston spent a few hours looking through Cottrell's files and photographs, which she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. Cottrell explained the organization and highlights of the collection. A video camera was left on to record the conversation, which could contain helpful insight to those who later want to use the collection. At the end, Cottrell says "They need to be turned loose. I'm glad we're turning them loose on Cornell, but I'm like, man! You know, who's going to be lining up at Cornell to see these things?"

p.extref: An excerpt of the interview can be found here:

scopecontent.p: Description of Excerpt: September 6, 2015. Talking about her vision for the first issue of On Our Backs and the psycho-sexual subway map of urban lesbian life she created. Shows an ad for her erotic photography and reflects on the vision for both On Our Backs and Countervision. Highlights of her commentary as we look at the photographs and files she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. (Talking with Brenda Marston.) 13:54 minutes.

did.unittitle: Photo backup: muertos, Jackie, la Boheme, war queer
